items.summary: Keep ContextLattice native memory plugin and provider discovery in the Rust agent runtime.
items.rationale: This is an intentional Rust-native enhancement that improves memory orchestration and does not replace or remove upstream behavior.

items.summary: Preserve webhook-driven sync queue worker architecture with sqlite, SQS, and Kafka support.
items.rationale: Queue backend plurality (sqlite/SQS/Kafka) is an ultra reliability extension for upstream sync maintenance and does not reduce upstream parity scope.

items.summary: Preserve launchd-based interactive dev lifecycle management for webhook listener and worker.
items.rationale: launchd lifecycle is a macOS operational hardening layer for development upkeep and is intentionally additive.

items.summary: Track upstream skills and optional-skills catalogs via parity audits while keeping Rust runtime skill loading externalized (no direct Python skill-tree vendoring).
items.rationale: Skills parity is delivered by catalog governance and runtime contract equivalence rather than vendoring Python implementation trees in a Rust-first repo.

items.summary: Keep platform adapter parity in the Rust gateway instead of vendoring upstream Python platform-plugin trees.
items.rationale: Platform behavior is represented by Rust-native gateway adapters, delivery helpers, auth/config readers, and adapter contracts; upstream Python plugin files are reference-only and must not become runtime execution paths in the Rust-first repo.

items.summary: Own Chronos managed-cron behavior in the Rust cron scheduler and API server instead of vendoring the upstream Python cron plugin.
items.rationale: Hermes Ultra implements NAS-mediated Chronos provisioning, callback JWT verification, stale/duplicate fire suppression, background dispatch, and post-fire re-arm in hermes-cron plus the Rust API server. The upstream plugins/cron Python files are reference-only and must not become Rust runtime execution paths.

items.summary: Track the upstream Raft Python platform plugin as non-runtime until a Rust-native Raft adapter is scoped.
items.rationale: No Rust gateway Raft adapter is registered locally. Ultra preserves Rust-only runtime behavior and does not vendor the upstream Python wake-bridge adapter; this remains an explicit platform-gap item rather than an unclassified missing plugin tree.

items.summary: Track upstream Python backend plugin trees that are not yet closed as Rust-native runtime surfaces.
items.rationale: dashboard_auth/nous, dashboard_auth/drain, and security-guidance remain upstream-only Python plugin trees after this review. Hermes Ultra keeps runtime behavior Rust-native; these paths stay tracked for dedicated parity slices instead of being silently vendored.

items.summary: Do not import upstream implementation-plan archive documents unless they change local runtime or release behavior.
items.rationale: The upstream s6-overlay plan is a post-implementation reference document for upstream Docker/Python service-management history; Hermes Ultra tracks relevant runtime parity through local Docker, gateway, and release gates.

items.summary: Treat Rust CLI/TUI and gateway as primary UX surface; upstream web/ui-tui trees are tracked as intentional divergence unless explicitly ported.
items.rationale: User-facing runtime parity is provided via Rust CLI/TUI/gateway. Web/ui-tui trees remain tracked and auditable, but not auto-vendored.

items.summary: Keep Honcho and Mem0 memory provider behavior in Rust-native memory/provider surfaces instead of vendoring Python plugin trees.
items.rationale: Honcho and Mem0 are tracked as rust-native memory_plugin entries in the adapter feature matrix and covered by Rust memory/config contracts; upstream Python plugin files are reference-only.

items.summary: Keep OpenRouter and Krea image generation provider behavior in the Rust image backend instead of vendoring upstream Python plugin trees.
items.rationale: crates/hermes-tools/src/backends/image_gen.rs implements OpenRouter-compatible image output, Nous-compatible routing, and Krea direct/managed transports with Rust tests; upstream Python image plugin files are reference-only.
